title: Introduction to Sociolinguistics
outline: This course focuses on the study of language in its social context, a branch of Linguistics that is referred to as sociolinguistics. The course includes topics such as introduction (b asic concepts and issues in sociolinguistics); regional variation; social variation; language change, language and social media; multilingualism; language an d interaction; gender and languag e; language contact; pidgins, creoles and new Englishes; language planning and policy; language and education; the sociolinguistics of sign language.
lecture_times: See module block schedule and official class timetable.
assessment: Continuous assessment (essays, projects, tests, etc.) counts 100%.
